etymology.description: 凑(湊) (còu, "gather; put together") suggests the idea of bringing elements together, often with the sense of improvisation or making do. 火(火) (huǒ, "fire") here metaphorically represents cooking or preparing food, as fire is essential for cooking. Combining 凑(湊) and 火, the phrase illustrates the concept of hurriedly gathering and preparing a meal.
etymology.interpretation: In the expression 湊湊火 (còu còu huǒ), the sense of 'make do with a meal' is captured by the notion of 凑(湊) (còu, "gather")—implying hastily collecting resources or ingredients. 火(火) (huǒ, "fire") is symbolic of cooking or the act of preparing food. The phrase thus conveys the idea of quickly putting together a meal using whatever is available, akin to making do with the resources at hand.
